code:
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment based solely on the review text
    - Base rating on holistic satisfaction, not isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1★ (Very Negative):
    - Safety hazards/multi-purchase failures overriding positives
    - Fundamental usability failures despite concept appeal
    - Explicit "avoid purchase" warnings with defect evidence
    - Negative updates erase previous satisfaction

    2★ (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires excessive workarounds
    - Partial functionality with persistent frustration
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - "Regret purchase" statements with justification

    3★ (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Adequate performance with notable compromises
    - Target-specific adequacy without endorsement

    4★ (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Requires preprocessing/break-in for full function
    - Assembly difficulties despite final satisfaction
    - Brief factual praise lacking enthusiasm ("does the job")

    5★ (Very Positive):
    - Concise praise without negatives ("Fits perfectly")
    - 3+ functional aspects praised (durability/design/utility)
    - Solves persistent problems exceeding expectations
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise
    - Close comparisons to top brands with satisfaction
    - Effective core functionality meeting needs without defects

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Works as intended" →5 unless functional criticism exists
    - Material maintenance needs →5 if non-functional
    - Praise + functional flaw →4 / + non-functional →5
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - "Nearly matches X" comparisons →5 with satisfaction
    - Core function achieved post-setup →5 if flawless
    - Brief effectiveness praise →5 even if concise
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures override positives →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Does exactly what needed - deep pockets fit any mattress"
<Reasoning>Concise functional praise without negatives →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Nearly matches Lodge! Daily driver despite minor differences"
<Reasoning>Close brand comparison with satisfaction →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Good storage bin - not extraordinary but keeps items organized"
<Reasoning>Effective core functionality without defects →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "These towels need 2 washes but work well after"
<Reasoning>Functional prerequisite impacts experience →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Sharp tablecloth. Only con is inherent need to iron cotton"
<Reasoning>Non-functional criticism with core praise →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Works but constantly needs readjusting. Exhausting"
<Reasoning>Excessive workarounds →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Material feels cheap but holds items adequately"
<Reasoning>Functional adequacy with compromise →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "UPDATE: Failed after 2 months. Initial satisfaction erased"
<Reasoning>Negative update overriding previous experience →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Fits mattresses adequately. Does the job"
<Reasoning>Factual description lacking enthusiasm →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (29 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 17
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 1 -> 2: 3
  gold 2 -> 1: 1
  gold 5 -> 4: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "I would have given this a five star rating had it fit the appliance it was purchased for. It didn't. With that being said, make sure you are positive of the model number of your ice tea maker before ordering and don't order based on how it "looks". H..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "Since buying this product and it dying 12 months later I've owned numerous carpet cleaners including the Bissel Proheat 2X Pet Lift Off and the at-home version of the Rug Doctor. Both of these blew the performance of this machine out of the water. Th..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 1 | Workers: [1, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "This looks like a great idea and easy to use, but actually it's very awkward. The idea is to hang your jewelry in the closet. But the hanger part doesn't swivel, so if you want to select an item, you have to remove it from the closet and lay it on th..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [1, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "This machine seems determined to foil every effort I make to clean my carpets. The bottom section never works. It's job is to collect the dirty water, but since the top section hardly puts any water out at all, it doesn't have much to do. I have labo..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [1, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "Between a puppy and frequent visits from children, I've definitely had times when it felt like the gap between proper carpet cleanings was entirely too long. On the other hand, I never entirely bought the idea that a device of this type could turn ou..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 29 errors, 20 borderline out of 80 examples."}
